text: BREAKING: Iranian IRGC drone struck fully laden Kuwaiti VLCC 'Al-Salmi' at anchor in Dubai port area just after midnight local time. Fire on hull. All 24 crew evacuated safely. First-ever Iranian strike on a commercial vessel in port (vs at sea). Oil spill risk from fully loaded tanker. IRGC claims Israeli-linked flag. Dubai government and Kuwait Petroleum Corp confirm. Separately: 3 Chinese-flagged vessels transited Hormuz unopposed today — selective blockade continues. Total crossings since March 1: ~195 (down 95% from peacetime).
verificationNote: Al-Salmi attack confirmed by Bloomberg and Al Jazeera. Chinese vessel transit confirmed by CNN liveblog.

text: Day 31 late PM summary (5:39 PM PDT): (1) IRGC Commander Hassanzadeh killed in Tehran; (2) Gen. Zakariaei dies from March 28 wounds; (3) 82nd Airborne arrives — 57,000+ US troops; (4) NATO 4th missile interception over Turkey; (5) Saudi intercepts 5 missiles; (6) Kuwait camp hit, 10 soldiers hurt; (7) Abu Dhabi debris injures 6; (8) Israeli strike kills LAF soldier; (9) IMF: largest oil disruption ever; (10) Knesset $45.8B defense budget; (11) Al-Salmi tanker struck in Dubai port.
verificationNote: Compiled from all verified sources above.

text: IDF announces 170-target strike wave across Tehran completed overnight. Targets include plutonium production facility, IRGC Imam Hussein University, missile R&D, UAV engine factory. Power blackout in Tehran from electrical infrastructure hits. IAF now claims 'air supremacy in most of Iran's airspace' after destroying 100+ air defense systems.
verificationNote: Confirmed by CBS News live updates, JNS, and Israel.com.

text: 4 IDF Nahal Brigade soldiers killed in Hezbollah ambush in southern Lebanon. Anti-tank missile hit during evacuation. Cpt. Madmoni (22), SSgt. Cohen (21), SSgt. Entis (21) + 1 named. Deadliest single IDF engagement in Lebanon since war began. IDF responded with tank fire + airstrikes. Separately, Haifa Bazan refinery gasoline tank hit by missile — fire contained.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog, Ynet, and Haaretz.

text: WSJ: Trump tells aides willing to end war without reopening Hormuz. Assessed reopening would extend conflict beyond 4-6 week timeline. Brent immediately dips ~1% to $111.55 on report. Separately, FT reports Hegseth's broker attempted multimillion defense ETF investment BEFORE war — Pentagon denies. Gulf states (Saudi, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain) privately told White House they want war to continue until regime change.
verificationNote: WSJ exclusive per Haaretz and US News. FT Hegseth report per CNBC and Al Jazeera. Gulf states per WSJ.

text: NYT: Killing dozens of Iranian leaders has hampered Tehran's decision-making AND complicates US negotiation efforts. Leadership decapitation empowering IRGC hardliners. Rubio-Hegseth 'at each other's throats' over troop deployment. Pakistan FM Dar in Beijing — China's Wang Yi backs mediation efforts.
verificationNote: NYT per Times of Israel liveblog. Rubio-Hegseth tensions per Middle East Eye. Pakistan-China per Al Jazeera.

text: Day 31-32 overnight/early AM summary (9:57 PM PDT): (1) IDF 170-target Tehran wave completed — plutonium facility + IRGC university; (2) 4 IDF soldiers killed in Lebanon Hezbollah ambush; (3) Haifa Bazan refinery hit again — gasoline tank fire; (4) Orphanage struck in Alborz — 2 killed; (5) Iran missile salvo at Jerusalem 5:30 AM — intercepted; (6) WSJ: Trump may end war without Hormuz; (7) FT: Hegseth investment scandal; (8) Brent $111.55; gas $3.99.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Haaretz, Al Jazeera, CNBC, CBS News, JNS, WSJ, FT.

text: BREAKING: Iranian missile with cluster munition warhead struck central Israel — bomblets over Bnei Brak, Ramat Gan, Petah Tikva. 12 wounded including 6 children. First confirmed cluster munition use by Iran against civilian areas. HRW documenting.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Ynet and HRW documentation.

text: Day 32 update: Iranian drone struck Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai anchorage ~00:10 local — 2M barrels crude, fire contained, 24 crew safe. Separately, IRGC Brig. Gen. Hassanzadeh (Rasul-Allah Corps Tehran) confirmed killed. Saudi intercepted 10 drones + 8 missiles overnight.
verificationNote: Al-Salmi: Bloomberg, Al Jazeera. Hassanzadeh: Alma Center, Israel National News. Saudi: CNN.

displayName: Joe Truzman — FDD Long War Journal
text: 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in south Lebanon within 24 hours — first UNIFIL fatalities of this conflict. France demands emergency UNSC meeting. IDF says investigating. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims 290+ attacks on US forces since war began.
verificationNote: UNIFIL: France 24, UN News. Iraq: FDD Long War Journal.

text: Iran Parliament approves Hormuz toll plan: rial-denominated fees, US/Israeli ships banned, sanctioning nation ships banned. Ghalibaf says he has intel on US plan to seize an Iranian island with regional ally. Revenue potential $600-800M/month. Rubio: 'illegal, unacceptable, dangerous.'
verificationNote: BNN Bloomberg and India TV confirmed parliamentary approval.

text: Rubio tells Al Jazeera: war objectives 'weeks not months.' Confirms backchannel with Iran via intermediaries. 'Would we be heartbroken if there was regime change? Absolutely not.' Separately: Pope Leo on Palm Sunday rejected God-justifies-war claims, drawing sharp contrast with Trump administration.
verificationNote: Rubio: Al Jazeera interview + State Dept transcript. Pope: NPR, Axios.

text: Day 32 AM summary (4:31 AM PDT): (1) Cluster munition hits central Israel — 12 wounded; (2) IRGC Gen. Hassanzadeh killed; (3) Al-Salmi tanker attacked at Dubai; (4) 3 UNIFIL killed — first fatalities; (5) Saudi intercepts 18 projectiles; (6) Iran toll plan approved; (7) Rubio: 'weeks not months'; (8) Gas $4.02; Brent $112.90; (9) Knesset $45.8B budget.
verificationNote: Compiled from Ynet, Bloomberg, Alma Center, France 24, CNN, BNN Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, AAA, Breaking Defense.
